threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn’t call out every stop. In Clapton, it’s all about the silence between stops.
Caught a whiff of jerk chicken from a street vendor on the 37 last night; felt like a mini carnival at 2 AM with everyone sharing stories.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy in a full tuxedo buying döner at 3 AM outside the U-Bahn. That's when I knew this place never sleeps.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do the kebab shops stay open all night, blending Turkish döner with midnight techno beats. It's a vibe unique to Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they know which taco truck has the late-night lengua special, while the newcomers just Google everything.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy strumming a ukulele on the 26 Valencia, while someone else tried to sell a broken selfie stick. Just another day.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's all about Gaudí, but it’s really about the local bars where the only sculpture is a good tapa and a bottle of vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their constant confusion over the ‘tapa’ sizes; they're still ordering full plates when the rest of us know to ask for half.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'please' and 'thank you' to the bus driver, expecting a reply. Around here, you just nod and keep it moving.
Caught a glimpse of a guy in a dinosaur onesie trying to woo a girl with a ukulele on the 59 last night. Classic Brixton vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I belonged was when I found a random mural of a cat in a sombrero on a side street. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a club in a former swimming pool, complete with diving boards and an actual slide. It's a unique mix of nightlife and nostalgia!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their disdain for the Sunday farmers' market crowds but still showing up for the fresh chilaquiles.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 14 Mission, eating takeout from a carton. Only in SF do you dress sharp and keep it casual at the same time.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the squares and terraces, but it’s the narrow alleys and hidden bars that hold the real charm and community vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell them by the way they stare at the metro map—like it's a puzzle instead of just getting off at Diagonal.
